flashcards.front: Limitation period for Demand Promissory Note
flashcards.back: 3 years from the date of the note.
A bank or financial institution in whose favor a security interest is created.
Empowers the secured creditor to take possession of the secured asset if the borrower fails to comply with the 60-day notice.
Power of RBI to give directions to banks in the interest of banking policy or depositors.
Deals with the dishonor of checks for insufficiency of funds; a criminal offense punishable by up to 2 years imprisonment or fine up to twice the check amount.
Banks must maintain records of transactions for at least 5 years from the date of the transaction.
Debt Recovery Tribunals (DRT) handle cases where the debt amount is ₹20 Lakh or more (reduced from earlier levels for specific cohorts).
The legal right of a bank to adjust the credit balance in one account against the debit balance in another account of the same customer.

questions.question: An appeal against the order of the DRT must be filed with the DRAT within how many days?
questions.explanation: Section 18 of the SARFAESI Act specifies that an appeal to the DRAT must be filed within 30 days of the DRT order.

questions.question: Which section of the RBI Act 1934 empowers the RBI to regulate the banking system in India?
questions.explanation: Section 17 of the RBI Act outlines the various types of banking business that the RBI can transact, effectively allowing it to regulate the system through its operations.
